name: Heading is descriptive
description: This rule checks that headings describe the topic or purpose of the content.
applicability_text: This rule applies to any [semantic][semantic role] `heading` element that is [included in the accessibility tree][] and has a non-empty (`””`) [accessible name][].
expectations:
Each target element describes the topic or purpose of the first [perceivable content][] after the test target that is not [decorative][]. The order of elements is determined by the [flat tree][].

**Note:** Headings do not need to be lengthy. A word, or even a single character, may be sufficient.

examples.name: Passed Example 1
examples.description: This `h1` heading element describes the topic of the following paragraph.
examples.code:
Opening Hours

We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

examples.name: Passed Example 2
examples.description: This heading marked up with an [explicit role][] of `heading` describes the topic of the following paragraph.
examples.code:
Opening Hours

We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

examples.name: Passed Example 3
examples.description: This `h1` heading element with an image describes the topic of the following paragraph.
examples.code: We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

examples.name: Passed Example 4
examples.description: This `h1` heading element has a single character text that describes the topic of the following description list.
examples.code:
A

airplane
a powered flying vehicle with fixed wings and a weight greater than that of the air it displaces.
apple
the round fruit of a tree of the rose family, which typically has thin green or red skin and crisp flesh.

examples.name: Passed Example 5
examples.description: This heading marked up with an [explicit role][] of `heading` describes the topic of the following paragraph. The heading is positioned off screen but is [included in the accessibility tree][].
examples.code:
Opening Hours

We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

examples.name: Passed Example 6
examples.description: This heading describes the first [perceivable content][] after it (the first `p` element). The next [perceivable content][] (the second `p` element) is not considered by this rule.
examples.code:
Opening Hours

We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

We are open Saturday from 10 to 13

examples.name: Failed Example 1
examples.description: This `h1` heading element does not describes the topic of the following paragraph.
examples.code:
Weather

We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

examples.name: Failed Example 2
examples.description: This heading marked up with an [explicit role][] of `heading` does not describe the topic of the following paragraph.
examples.code:
Weather

We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

examples.name: Failed Example 3
examples.description: This heading marked up with an [explicit role][] of `heading` does not describe the topic of the following paragraph. The heading is positioned off screen but is [included in the accessibility tree][].
examples.code:
Weather

We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

examples.name: Failed Example 4
examples.description: This `h1` heading element does not describe the first [perceivable content][] after it (the first `p` element). The next [perceivable content][] (the second `p` element) is not considered by this rule.
examples.code:
Weather

We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

It is going to rain tomorrow

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 1
examples.code: We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 2
examples.description: This `h1` heading element is not [included in the accessibility tree][].
examples.code:
Opening Hours

We are open Monday through Friday from 10 to 16

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 3
examples.description: This `h1` heading element has an empty [accessible name][].

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 4
examples.description: Semantic heading has an empty [accessible name][].

background: Headings that are visible but not in the accessibility tree are a failure of [Success Criterion 1.3.1 Info and Relationships][sc131]. These are not tested by this rule but they can still fail [Success Criterion 2.4.6 Headings and Labels][sc246].
assumptions:
This rule assumes that the [flat tree][] order is close to the reading order as elements are rendered on the page. Due to positioning, it is possible to render a document in an order that greatly differs from the tree order, in which case the content which is visually associated with a heading might not be the content following it in tree order and this rule might fail while [Success Criterion 2.4.6 Headings and Labels][sc246] is still satisfied.

This rule also assumes that the content the heading is intended to describe is [visible][] and not hidden from assistive technologies. Otherwise, cases such as expandable content using a heading might fail this rule while [Success Criterion 2.4.6 Headings and Labels][sc246] is still satisfied.
accessibility_support: Implementation of [Presentational Roles Conflict Resolution][] varies from one browser or assistive technology to another. Depending on this, some [semantic][semantic role] `heading` elements can fail this rule with some technology but users of other technologies would not experience any accessibility issue.
